description: Cross-language parity vectors for the canonical leaderboard scorer. Expected scores are computed by the Python canonical scorer (policybench.analysis) and replayed through the TypeScript scorer (app/src/lib/canonicalScore.ts) in app/tests/canonicalScore.test.ts.

vectors.description: Full program set, all reference cases — the canonical headline path with no filtering.
vectors.name: unfiltered_full_exact

vectors.description: Full program set, all reference cases — the canonical headline path with no filtering.
vectors.name: unfiltered_full_within1pct

vectors.description: Full program set, all reference cases — the canonical headline path with no filtering.
vectors.name: unfiltered_full_continuous

vectors.description: One active output group; weights renormalize to it.
vectors.name: single_program_exact

vectors.description: One active output group; weights renormalize to it.
vectors.name: single_program_within1pct

vectors.description: One active output group; weights renormalize to it.
vectors.name: single_program_continuous

vectors.description: Reference filter = positives; rows are subset before the canonical scorer runs.
vectors.name: positives_only_exact

vectors.description: Reference filter = positives; rows are subset before the canonical scorer runs.
vectors.name: positives_only_within1pct

vectors.description: Reference filter = positives; rows are subset before the canonical scorer runs.
vectors.name: positives_only_continuous

vectors.description: Reference filter = zeros; rows are subset before the canonical scorer runs.
vectors.name: zeros_only_exact

vectors.description: Reference filter = zeros; rows are subset before the canonical scorer runs.
vectors.name: zeros_only_within1pct

vectors.description: Reference filter = zeros; rows are subset before the canonical scorer runs.
vectors.name: zeros_only_continuous

vectors.description: Every amount reference is 0; exercises the zero-reference row-score branch (exact-zero credit).
vectors.name: all_zero_reference_exact

vectors.description: Every amount reference is 0; exercises the zero-reference row-score branch (exact-zero credit).
vectors.name: all_zero_reference_within1pct

vectors.description: Every amount reference is 0; exercises the zero-reference row-score branch (exact-zero credit).
vectors.name: all_zero_reference_continuous

vectors.description: Some (model, row) cells are absent from predictions; the dense payload represents them as null-prediction cells that score 0 but still carry weight.
vectors.name: models_missing_rows_exact

vectors.description: Some (model, row) cells are absent from predictions; the dense payload represents them as null-prediction cells that score 0 but still carry weight.
vectors.name: models_missing_rows_within1pct

vectors.description: Some (model, row) cells are absent from predictions; the dense payload represents them as null-prediction cells that score 0 but still carry weight.
vectors.name: models_missing_rows_continuous

vectors.description: globalWeights omits one present group and includes phantom groups absent from the data; the present unweighted group's rows get weight 0 while the rest of the household stays positively weighted.
vectors.name: weights_groups_mismatch_exact

vectors.description: globalWeights omits one present group and includes phantom groups absent from the data; the present unweighted group's rows get weight 0 while the rest of the household stays positively weighted.
vectors.name: weights_groups_mismatch_within1pct

vectors.description: globalWeights omits one present group and includes phantom groups absent from the data; the present unweighted group's rows get weight 0 while the rest of the household stays positively weighted.
vectors.name: weights_groups_mismatch_continuous

vectors.description: Active program is a single zero-population-weight output group, so every household's renormalization denominator is 0. Pins the documented Python/TypeScript divergence.
vectors.divergenceReason: A household's entire kept active set carries zero output-group weight, so the within-household renormalization denominator is 0. Python counts the household in the equal-household mean (score 0); the TypeScript scorer skips it (denominator <= 0 guard). Reachable in production via a zero-population-weight output group such as local_income_tax (US).
vectors.name: zero_weight_single_program_exact

Reachable in production via a zero-population-weight output group such as local_income_tax (US).
